Cooperation Protocol Signed with the Family Mediation Association

15.01.2026

The cooperation protocol signed between our University's Dispute Resolution Application and Research Center and the Family Mediation Association provides an institutional framework for family-focused work through education, research, and joint activities.

A cooperation protocol was signed between our University's Dispute Resolution Application and Research Center and the Family Mediation Association on 12 January, 2026, Monday. This cooperation aimed at strengthening the family structure and bringing together academic knowledge and field experience.

The cooperation protocol was signed by Attorney-Mediator Ayşe Dilek Ergüler, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Family Mediation Association, Attorney-Mediator Nur Özden, Vice Chair of the Board of Directors, and Prof. Dr. Yeliz Bozkurt Gümrükçüoğlu, Director of the Dispute Resolution Application and Research Center and Vice Rector of our university.

Under the signed protocol, the aim is to carry out education, research, seminars, workshops, and joint projects in the field of family mediation, to bring academic studies together with practical applications, and to implement activities aimed at raising social awareness.

This collaboration aims to leverage the academic expertise of our university and the field experience of the Family Mediation Association through joint projects and to contribute to family-focused social policies.

Our university will continue to support initiatives centered on family, society, and social structure, with its approach that is sensitive to social needs, application-oriented, and prioritizes stakeholder collaboration.
